entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line – High Level Introduction
entry.content:
In this blog, I will offer a glimpse into the near future of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which will introduce a new approach to managing special testing topics to support a public cloud deployment (SAP Labs Preview Only) using a concept called the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line.

Before we jump to the future, I would like to provide a high-level overview of some of the most common SAP tasks, such as data migration, sandbox setup, and data refresh, and how you can achieve them today with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Client copies and client refreshes, while common practices in on-premise environments (such as SAP ERP 6.0 or SAP S/4HANA), are not included in the standardized operational procedure design or SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Some customers and partners, influenced by traditional on-premise tasks, may believe they can perform client copies, either local or remote, as they do on-premise. However, they later realize this is not possible.

As explained in some of my other blogs, such as Accelerated Business Transformation – Moving from SAP ERP 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, has a different system design (e.g., multi-tenancy, SaaS, operations provided by SAP, security considerations, etc.). In the public cloud, we offer different methods to implement and operate the system compared to the traditional on-premise counterparts. This distinction is crucial to ensure system readiness for continuous innovation upgrades and to maintain high standards of operational procedures.

Data Migration: The execution of data migration in the public cloud today is outlined in SAP Activate and utilizes the SAP Data Migration Cockpit. This process is primarily carried out using the concept of delta migration. We recommend our customers first simulate the migration and then perform delta migration tests until everything is ready and verified. Thousands of customers have successfully followed this approach. For additional information, you can check the following links: https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/11/c076b9ef-967e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html  & https://www.sap.com/documents/2020/07/7a263781-a67d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html

There are times when customers and partners wish to perform data migration mock-up loads, where they load data and then delete (or refresh) it in test or productive systems. This allows them to conduct as many full mock-up loads as needed or to correct any data loaded incorrectly. Unfortunately, this traditional practice is not included in our standardized operational procedures, so it cannot be performed. Therefore, it is advisable to always plan migrations using delta loads with simulation in mind, as described above.

SAP Data Migration Cockpit

Sandbox:  We offer our customers the option to subscribe to a Sandbox; however, unlike traditional basis tasks, you cannot copy your development or testing system or move configuration transports to a SAP S/4HANA Public Edition - Sandbox. While we provide the ability to have a Sandbox in the public cloud, the Sandbox remains independent and aligns more with the concept of "fit to standard." This means the Sandbox becomes a reference system for SAP Best Practices that you can use to check and test. If customers want the Sandbox to more closely resemble their current development or testing system, they can configure and maintain the Sandbox, but this will require them to keep it up-to-date with their current configuration settings and data. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition follows a fit-to-standard approach, I recommend using the Sandbox primarily as a reference system for SAP Best Practices. This will allow you to explore innovations and have an environment to simulate or test extensibility. The Sandbox also offers the advantage of allowing you to test scope item elements that might require an additional subscription, among other features. However, if you have a more complex scenario for a Sandbox, such as implementing a non-standard country using the CBC - Configuration Localization Toolkit, you may need to configure the Sandbox system accordingly.

Data Refresh: In the public cloud, we offer the Test Data Refresh (TDR) service. This service has an additional subscription cost and allows our customers to refresh production data (both master and transactional) to the test system on a predefined schedule that aligns with the public cloud maintenance schedule (including upgrades and hotfix collections). The service also provides the capability for data depersonalization, which is not possible with client copies. An additional advantage compared to a system copy is that no tedious post-refresh activities are required by the customer. Integrations continue to work as before since the refresh occurs at the database level. This service is an excellent alternative for running regression testing for upgrades or future rollouts using data that closely resembles production data. You can read more about the SAP TDR service here.

SAP Test Data Refresh – Check Your System Readiness App

Looking ahead - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line is a state-of-the-art offering that provides enhanced testing flexibility in the public cloud using a parallel project line in addition to the main project line (see figure below). We will provide more information about the Parallel Project Line when it becomes generally available in the future, which is tentatively expected—subject to change—in the first half of 2025.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line

We are planning to provide customers with one parallel project line as part of their current SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition three-system landscape, parallel project line was created primarily for live customers to allow them a clear and flawless separation of activities in the "main line"—the line of their productive operations. This is especially useful during lifecycle events like upgrades, which bring new innovations to the productive environment. Customers can test and validate these innovations in a project line without impacting the main line of business operations. This includes scenarios such as the extension of new scopes or the rollout of new countries. Live customers can also use it for additional testing and validation of subsequent rollout waves or for proof of concepts or exploration. Customers who are not yet live and are working on their first implementation wave can also use it for data migration tests and ad-hoc training and testing environments. Therefore, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel project line will provide ample flexibility to customers at any stage of their lifecycle.

To summarize, this new offering will allow us to introduce more testing flexibility into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including but not limited to:

A) Scope extensions in the parallel project line without impacting the main line, with the option to test and validate them in the parallel project line and then merge them back to the main line if successful.

B) Rollout of new non-standard countries using the Central Business Configuration – Configuration Localization Toolkit. The parallel project line will help test the new non-standard countries without the need for a Sandbox.

C) Organizational Structure Extension: Similar to scope extensions, customers will be able to test and validate organizational structure extensions in the parallel project line and then merge them back to the main line if successful.

D) Data Migration: You will be able to perform additional mock-up data migration loads with the ability to refresh (clear) the project line.

E) Build Integration: You will be able to test integrations using the parallel project line.

F) Ad-hoc Training & Testing Environment: You will be able to create an ad-hoc training or testing environment as needed.

G) Data Refresh to Parallel Project Line: The SAP Test Data Refresh Service is planned to be offered for the project line as well.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line configuration – Organizational Structure Extension in Central Business Configuration.

I hope this content helps you understand how we plan to approach new testing methods that previously required traditional client copies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition three-system landscape, parallel line as you prepare for your project.

I would like to thank my colleagues, Sophia Sommerrock and Nicola Pace, for their review and feedback on this blog.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by adding comments to this blog.

More Information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, release info here
    • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
    • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
    • Early Release Webinar Series here
    • Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast here
    • openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA here
    • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, here
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Community: here
    • Feature Scope Description here
    • What’s New here
    • Help Portal Product Page here

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia

entry.title: How to Route Assignment Approvers for Technical Roles When a Business Role is Created or Modified?
entry.content:
Hi experts!

 

I am seeking guidance on configuring a workflow in SAP GRC 10 to handle assignment approvers for technical roles that are newly added to a business role when it is created or modified.

Example:

 

  • If ROLE_TYPE = SIN (Simple Role):
    • The workflow should follow the standard approval process.
  • If ROLE_TYPE = BUS (Business Role):
    • The system should identify which technical roles are associated with the business role and handle them differently:
      • Existing Technical Roles (already assigned to the business role): No workflow should be triggered.
      • New Technical Roles (recently added to the business role): A workflow should be triggered that:
        1. Sends the request to the Assignment Approver for the technical role.
        2. After approval by the Assignment Approver, routes the request to the Business Role Content Approver.

Thanks in advance for your help!

#sap GRC #brf+ #msmp workflow #BRM AC

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA picking up onboarding users
entry.content: @abalasescu2 - WFA reports all the data that is available in Job Info table. If there is already a record in Job Info while the employee still doing onboarding, you need to add exclusion logic. You need to find out what/which field will tell you if this employee still onboarding or not and use it to exclude.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adaptation Transport Organizer
entry.content:
Hi Sathya_Gunasekaran

Ok so Custom Fields are always cross-client. So you only need to create it in one client and it applies to all clients. 

However for all such Key User activities - and remember key users are CRITICAL to Clean Core - you MUST have a working SAP Fiori environment with some data where you can run the apps and  make these key user changes.

In most cases we recommend using your Development "Unit Test" client. That is key user changes such as Custom Fields, Adapt Ui (e.g. to change visibility of fields, buttons, tabs, cards, etc. and to create public app views) are made in the Dev Unit Test client. Then usually you set up your transport landscape to:

a) Create customizing requests for Key User activities. Remember key users cannot create transport requests for themselves - so this is ALWAYS under your IT team's control. 

b) Transport these customizing requests from your Dev Unit Test back to your master config client

c) Then transport all customizing requests (key user and other config) to the rest of your landscape as normal.

Best

Jocelyn

entry.content:
Hi team.

I have the same problem that @fgfgfg3r43456534 

in my case y only create a demo code in a SE38 report using I_JOURNALENTRYTP 

    DATA lt_new TYPE TABLE FOR ACTION IMPORT i_journalentrytp~Post.

    INSERT VALUE #(
        %cid   = to_upper( cl_uuid_factory=>create_system_uuid( )->create_uuid_x16( ) )
        %param = VALUE #(
            companycode            = '1000'
            createdbyuser          = sy-uname
            accountingdocumenttype = 'SA'
            documentdate           = '20250108'
            postingdate            = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( )
            DocumentReferenceID    = |RAP: { sy-uname }|
            _glitems               = VALUE #(
*                ( glaccountlineitem = '1' glaccount = '0011002080' _currencyamount = VALUE #( ( journalentryitemamount = '-100.55' currency = 'EUR' ) ) )
                ( glaccountlineitem = '1' glaccount = '824004' CostCenter = '12008'  _currencyamount = VALUE #( ( journalentryitemamount = '-10.55' currency = 'ARS' ) ) )
*                ( glaccountlineitem = '2' glaccount = '0070200000' _currencyamount = VALUE #( ( journalentryitemamount = '100.55' currency = 'EUR' ) ) ) ) ) )
                ( glaccountlineitem = '2' glaccount = '130011' _currencyamount = VALUE #( ( journalentryitemamount = '10.55' currency = 'ARS' ) ) ) ) ) )

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_journalentrytp

entry.title: Solman requirements
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

 

We have only one Productive Solman system for all the Gerdau systems, so we don't have a test environment to perform these PERSON_NAME recommendations, so we have to be really careful, could you help me address the requirements bellow please ?

 

As part of SAP Health Checking/PERSON_NAME recommendations, we received a recommendation to enable SSL communication in our Solman system. 

 

First doubt: the recommendation refers to the Java SID of our Solman system (SJ0). However, we also have the ABAP SID (SM0)..
I am unsure if we should set up HTTPS only in Java or also in ABAP layer..

 

Also.. which caution do we have to take to implement this ? do we have to modify any RFCs on ABAP side ? do we have to modify any connections on Java
side ? which/where ? do we also have to create certificates signed by certification authority in other systems to accomplish this ?
Would we also require to implement SNC certificate on ABAP side or just TLS ?
Should we use the same certificates signed by certificate authority in JAVA and ABAP layer ?

 

The recommendation (JE143) says:
"SSL is not set up or the default(self-signed) certificate is used. Ensure secure communication by using SSL certificates only. 
A default certificate should be replaced by a "real" certificate, signed by a proper certification authority. After applying the recommendation for SSL configuration, several parameters should be adjusted accordingly".

 

It doesn't say which parameters have to be modified and it does not point to any KB..

 

“Please refer to the following two sections” => Then it points to other two recommendations(JE167 and JE166) that seems to the same (same description),
which require SSL to be implemented in advance from my understanding.

 

I can't tell the difference between these:
-Secure Attribute for Security Session ID Cookie (JE167)
-Secure Attribute for System Cookie (JE166). 

 

Description: "If the property is set to true, then the secure attribute is set to the system cookies and the cookies marked as secure will only be transmitted if the communications channel is a secure one (https)"

 

And we also have this one to implement:
-Enforce SSL encryption for Transfer of SAP Logon Tickets (JE139). 

 

Then it points to a KB that does not exist (DIGITS) - where do I find the corresponding steps ? Is it something to be prepared after implementing SSL or something to be done in parallel ? :

 

"Use SSL at least between the client and the first load balancer or reverse proxy.
See SAP Note DIGITS (Switching to HTTPS Transport Layer Security) and SAP Note DIGITS (SSL tips for preventing 
hijacking).
Use DNS aliases in a separate subdomain for accessing the portal and the integrated systems (See other issue for details).

 

After you have implemented SSL (at least between the browsers and the load balancers of the portal and all directly accessible integrated systems), set "ume.logon.security.enforce_secure_cookie" to "True", so that the SAP Logon Ticket can only be sent via HTTPS connections".

 


Please let me know,

 

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solman requirements
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

 

Could you please review the following KBAs:
510007 - Setting up SSL on Application Server ABAP
2445947 - [WEBINAR] Setting up SSL on NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP
1770585 - How to configure SSL on the AS Java

During Transaction SOLMAN_SETUP for Infrastructure Preparation your can configure HTTPs and SSL too, for example within Infrastructure Preparation, step Set Up Connectivity.

Later you may also get in touch with SSL if needed for other Solution Manager parts such as Diagnostic Agents and Enterprise Manager.
2322555 - Connect the Diagnostics Agent to Solution Manager 7.2 using SSL
2268643 - How to configure the P4S port with Solution Manager 7.2
2516194 - How to enable SSL Communication for Introscope Enterprise Manager and Introscope Host Adapters - SAP Solution Manager 7.1 and 7.2
2113268 - How to setup CA Introscope for SSL communication - SAP Solution Manager 7.1 and 7.2

 

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Create Subscription Quote Based On a Specific Configu...
entry.content:
Hi Avinash,

The configuration date has no influence on the conventional product bundle. With multi-AVC BOM, the BOM components are determined based on valid from date maintained in the BOM, like you have also highlighted. The components can either be object dependent, i.e. based on certain characteristic attribute of the BOM or they can be defined to explode always without object dependency. Later case is similar to conventional product bundle with configuration date (Valid from) flexibility.

As far as Subscription order creation is concerned, by default the current date is considered for contract start date because of standard date profile. You can create your own custom date profile where the contract start date can be influenced using a desired custom date rule. In your case, the custom date rule assigned against contract start date should copy the configuration date to it. Thanks.

Regards
Deepak

entry.title: SAP Field Logistics: Enhancing Rental Onboarding and Monitoring through SAP Fieldglass Integration
entry.content:
SAP Field Logistics is a solution built on SAP S/4HANA. It is available on S/4HANA Private Cloud, Public Cloud and On-Premise Editions. It allows customers to utilise best-in-class industry practices to manage logistics movement between their supply and demand plants.

SAP Fieldglass is a cloud-based vendor management system that streamlines procurement by managing external talent and services. It integrates effectively with SAP S/4HANA through SAP Business Network, offering a robust, best-practice service procurement process. This integration gives customers complete control and visibility over their source-to-pay process for services, ensuring seamless vendor collaboration.

For more information on the Service Procurement Integration, refer to the SAP help link: Integrations in the Sourcing and Procurement

Supplier Item Onboarding using Fieldglass

Customers frequently rent equipment for offshore operations, with demand typically linked to requests for service operations. Vendors usually recommend the necessary rental equipment and additional items, such as toolboxes, to support the job. ( These rental and third-party items are commonly referred to as Supplier Items). Efficient handling requires onboarding these supplier items with the service, monitoring their logistics, and ensuring timely offboarding.

Therefore, Field Logistics provides a ready-made standard integration between SAP Fieldglass and Field Logistics to facilitate the exchange of information about these items. This integration is designed with service procurement integration as a prerequisite, enhancing service procurement with additional tracking and management of rental and third-party items.

( Note: A reference business synopsis can be found here: Supplier Equipment Monitoring Business Synopsis )

Below is the high-level outline of the integration.

Integration Setup

1. Setup of the Service Procurement with SAP Business Network and SAP Fieldglass (4R2)

The customer must set up their system first for service procurement, following the best practices content for scope item 4R2. Refer to the documentation below to understand and set up the scenario.

Help info: Service Procurement with SAP Business Network and SAP Fieldglass (4R2)

Setting Up Service Procurement with SAP Business Network and SAP Fieldglass (4R2)

Config Guide: Services Procurement Implementation Guide

2. SAP Fieldglass setup for Supplier Item integration

The Fieldglass system must be set up with additional fields and connectors to make it ready for integration. The document below covers the setup steps to be performed.

Config Guide: Supplier Equipment Monitoring Implementation setup

3. Setup of the SAP Cloud Integration content

Standard integration flows facilitate information mapping and handover between SAP Fieldglass and Field Logistics systems. The customer must activate the content and perform an initial setup to enable the integration.

Config Guide: Configure the Integration flows and credentials

4. Setting up the Logistics execution for Supplier Items (4AH)

Customers must activate the Field Logistics process in the system to enable the logistics processes for supplier items. The setup guide below must be followed and completed to enable the best practice for scope item 4AH.

Config Guide: Setting Up Field Logistics Planning and Execution – Supplier Items (4AH)

Key Benefits

  1. Enhanced Visibility: Gain complete oversight of rental and third-party item lifecycles, including status updates and progress tracking.
  2. Improved Efficiency: Streamline the process of onboarding and managing rental and third-party items, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Accurate Cost Tracking: Ensure that the actual spending for billable days aligns with the required amounts, preventing overspending or underutilisation.
  4. Seamless Collaboration: Provide a centralised platform for managing rental and third-party items to facilitate collaboration between field service teams and suppliers.

Reference Information for SAP Field Logistics:

SAP Help Documentation:

SAP Field Logistics for any-premise

SAP Field Logistics for the public cloud

SAP Best practice content for S/4HANA Cloud for Supplier Item:

Supplier Item scope - 4AH

SAP Best practice content for S/4HANA Cloud for other Field Logistics scope:

Base Field Logistics Scope - 40G

Non-stock Item scope - 5OM

Container and Voyages – 6BA

Field Logistics Analytics – 6VA

 

Review Credits: Content reviewed and enriched by Field Logistics product and the UA team

entry.title: Re: RE-FX Intereses
entry.content:
Hi there are not too many options for the interest as this components is automatically calculated. You will need to fully understand how the calculation is done by the customer and then compare this with the calculation done in SAP. There is always the chance that the customer's calculation is wrong.

In the following note 2555105 - Explanation of calculations IFRS 16 / US GAAP ASC 842 you can find information about the calculation performed in SAP as well as the option to implement a BAdI for the interest calculation. Also have a look at note 3441594 - RE-FX: Leasing - interest calculation for proportional period.

Regards, Andre

entry.title: SAP Fiori elements extension: upload Excel files to create new entries using building blocks
entry.content:
I highly recommend reading this excellent blog that explains how to implement an SAP Fiori elements application with CAP Node.js and upload an Excel file to create entries in the backend. The blog uses pure UI5 coding for the upload functionality. At the time of writing, the SAP Fiori elements field building block was not yet released. In this blog, I will guide you through the process of implementing an SAP Fiori elements application by leveraging the newly released SAP Fiori elements field building block, which simplifies the implementation compared to the previous method. 

Implementation steps with the field building block 

With the release of the SAP Fiori elements field building block, we can now implement the upload functionality more efficiently. This new approach allows us to use the SAP Fiori elements field building block with a metapath to a stream property, making the implementation cleaner and more maintainable. 

In the referenced blog, you already learned how to add a custom action to your table. But instead of using the UI5 FileUploader, we use the SAP Fiori elements field building block to simplify the process: 

 

<mvc:View 
  xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" 
  xmlns="sap.m" 
  xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros" 
  controllerName="your.namespace.UploadDialog"> 

  <Dialog 
    id="uploadDialog" 
    title="Upload Excel File" 
    contentWidth="500px" 
    contentHeight="300px"> 

    <content> 
        <macros:Field id="excelFile" metaPath="/ExcelUpload/excel" /> 
    </content> 

    <beginButton> 
      <Button text="Close" press="closeDialog" /> 
    </beginButton> 

  </Dialog> 
</mvc:View>

 

And here's the controller so the user can close the dialog after upload: 

 

import Controller from "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"; 
import Dialog from "sap/m/Dialog"; 

export default class UploadDialog extends Controller { 
  closeDialog(): void { 
    const dialog = this.getView().byId("uploadDialog") as Dialog; 
    dialog.close(); 
  } 
}

 

SAP Fiori elements field building block streamlines the upload process 

As you can see in the XML we simply use the SAP Fiori elements field building block with the metapath to a stream property. This allows us to handle the upload functionality in a more streamlined manner. 

As the referenced blog uses a singleton to upload the file, we point to a singleton property by using an absolute path (see https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/buildingBlocks/guidance/singletonDefault to get more information on singleton usage). 

 

<macros:Field id="excelUpload" metaPath="/ExcelUpload/excel"/>

 

Using the SAP Fiori elements field building block simplifies implementation of upload functionality  

By using the SAP Fiori elements field building block, we can simplify the implementation of the upload functionality in an SAP Fiori elements application with CAP Node.js. This new approach is more efficient and maintainable compared to the previous method using pure UI5 coding.

Normally it is fine to turn off when doing "big" activities, however the concequence if we are bad luck, the system crashed during archived log off, the system may not be recoveriable. -> Means there is no log to do recovery.

entry.title: Product-Centric Pricing Harmonization Two Tier ERP
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post will detail the steps for Pricing Master integration in a Two-Tier scenario. The condition Pricing/Discounts condition for Sales created at the HQ (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP) will be replicated to the subsidiary (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) as Purchasing Info  records/Discounts conditions.

Business Requirements

Headquarter, the tier-1 establishes the pricing structure and discount terms for their products, ensuring uniformity across all the distribution organizations. These prices and discount structure are then required to be communicated automatically and applied as purchase pricing at the subsidiary, i.e. tier-2 system, in order to eliminate need for subsequent manual corrective reconciliation or adjustments.

This solution has a better fitment for the scenario for Sales office as a subsidiary and for the Distribution ecosystem scenario  wherein prices, discounts  are managed  by the headquarter and are followed by the subsidiary and distributors who place orders with the headquarter.

Capabilities

Purchase Order (PO) at Subsidiary

At the tier-2 system sales office or a distributor creates a Purchase Order (PO)  on the tier-1 system essentially for documenting the intent to buy goods from the headquarter at the agreed-upon terms (including pricing, quantity, delivery schedules, etc.). In this scenario, the PO would reflect the net price/discounts after applying the price and discount managed by the headquarter.

Sales Order (SO) at Headquarter

The tier-1 system sets the price and offers a sales discount, seasonal discounts to the sales office or distributors at the tier-2 system. This price schema would be reflected in the Sales order created against the PO at the tier-1 system.

Note : The solution capability for pricing harmonization can be set up to work bidirectionally. Prices can be replicated from tier-2 to tier 1 system as well.

Set Up

1. Subscribe and Set up Event Mesh and create Queue in Event mesh 

https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/btp-configuration/set-up-sap-event-mesh?version=2024-12-16

2. Queue should be subscribed to Sales Pricing Condition Record Events

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/71703bca89464fa0a164163979327bf4.html?version=2023.002

https://api.sap.com/event/OP_SALESPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDEVENTS/overview

3. In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition Subscribe to Maintain Event Channel Binding to the Events for Sales Pricing Condition records.

4. In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition maintain Set up Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0294

5. Create an iFlow with AMQP Adaptor that gets triggered when message is received in Queue

6. The iFlow is custom and can be designed as per custom requirement. In this case to describe an event for create is triggered when sales pricing condition record is created in SAP S/4HANA Private cloud edition. Using this event the condition record is captured as part of event; we use this to do an API call in SAP S/4HANA Private cloud Edition to get all the pricing details. Then map this pricing details to make a post API call to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for creating Purchasing info record. Then we use the Purchase Info record number to make another API post call to create Purchasing Info Record Purch. Org. Data with pricing details.

7. Before Making Post call to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition make sure communication arrangements SAP_COM_0145 are active.

Process Overview

Conclusion

The blog post details the steps for Pricing Master integration in a Two-Tier scenario. 

 

__PRESENT

entry.title: Release Assessment and Scope Dependency for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: What is new in 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

To provide our customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with disruption free upgrades and zero day 1 impact, we have been tirelessly improving release assessment and scope dependency tool to meet the latest customer demands.

We have implemented several changes and enhancements in the release assessment and scope dependency tool. These modifications enable us to proactively inform our customers of all Day 1 impacts relevant to their usage.

Keep watching this space for more updates on release assessment and scope dependency coming in 2025.

Applications

With the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the what’s new viewer have introduced a new attribute called ‘Action’. This was introduced to address, which tasks that are communicated via what’s new, need to be focused/prioritized so that customers could fulfil the required actions in time. This will ensure that the urgency of the tasks are communicated appropriately.

The attribute ‘Action have the following values:

  • “Critical – Immediate Action Required” - Classification for all changes that cause a day-1 impact for the customer
  • “Action/Review Required” - Classification for all changes that improve an existing process but does not cause an immediate disruption while working with the affected functionality.
  • “Innovation” = Classification for all changes that enable a new functionality or process. Customers can decide not to use the innovation or to postpone the usage.
  • “Information” = Classification for changes with minor impact, for example, structural changes of the documentation, or changed shipment cycles.

Release assessment and scope dependency have adapted itself to accommodate these changes and the application tile have been redesigned as follows. This ensures that release assessment and scope dependency communicates the changes with appropriate urgency to our customers.

New Features

We have also consolidated the ‘New Features’ tile to include the number of features delivered in a particular release, to ease the consumption of the tool.

Conclusion

We are continuously trying to add more features to the tool so that upgrades are hassle free and our customer can derive more value out of their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation.

You can go thru our FAQ blog if you have any questions regarding release assessment and scope dependency.

entry.title: Re: Product Substitutions
entry.content: Hello Jurgen, Thanks for taking your time to go through the article. To answer your query, The Product / Location Substitution would get triggered only on ATP run on checking of availability and deficits. Incase the substitution material is maintained for the original/ requested material and if the substitution material is available then during case of orginal/ requested material is unavailable then during those cases, substitution material is proposed and displayed during 'Review Availability Check Results (RACR)'. On acceptance would get processed for next steps. But the substitution logic doesnt kick-up until ATP check is not done. Hope this explaination clarifies your query.

entry.title: Re: EWM SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, Warehouse Attr...
entry.content:
Hi,

The warehouse attributes can only be set once by our customer and cannot be changed anymore. So it needs to be very careful to select a correct UoM. If a wrong UoM is set (normally in the testing system), the customer normally copies another warehouse and set the UoM again. When it is the testing system and not so many movements, there is one option to open a ticket to ask the SAP team to change the UoM in the backend. Additionally, report needs to be run to correct the UoM of bin, HU, open WT...

The time unit I think is used in WT duration calculation (e.g. labor management). This is currently not available in the cloud WM.

entry.title: Re: SAP RISE Migration Options for Partner
entry.content:
Hi Charanjit, in the context of RISE :
1. You will need to add an App server as part of source system in Migration VM before you start the conversion. The  SUM upon starting will detects that, if it is not running on the Primary Application Server (PAS) host, it offers the ASCS instance move. This will install the ABAP central services on the AAS in the target, and the previous PAS will not be started again after downtime.

2. You may refer to SUM DMO SAP Notes 3429205 - Central Note - Software Update Manager 2.0 SP21 [lmt_021] - SAP for Me

Please have take a look on the SUM DMO Page Software Update Manager and System Move: Switching the Operating System | SAP Help Portal

and About this Document | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: EHS Duplicate Resource Error in Identify Risk app
entry.content:
Hi @Ali_Kaynee,

I guess the system you are currently working on is either a development or test system - hence, we can safely try different things to get rid of the duplicate in the identify risks app.

Let's start with the "normal" process: Assigning the identified risk to a risk assessment project.

  1. Open the app "Assign Risks to Projects" (F2646),
  2. You should be able to see the risk(s) causing the problem in the list,
  3. On the upper-right corner, click on "Create Risk Assessment Project",
  4. A pop-up will show up. Enter the information about the risk assessment to be created: Assessment Type: JHA (Job Hazard Analysis), Job: General Engineer, Location: 10-A-8005 A, Operational Status: Normal Operation. The Job, Location, and Operational Status must match the one of the identified risk. Enter title as per your preference.
  5. In the list, select the "Corrosion" risk, and click on the button in the upper-right corner "Assign to Risk Assessment Project", pick the risk assessment project you created in step 4.
  6. Go back to the app "Identify Risks" (F2411), make sure that the risk is no longer shown.
  7. You can now open the created risk assessment project either from the app "My risk assessment projects" (F1999), or from the app "Manage risk assessment projects".
  8. In the tab "risks" you can either handle the risk as required, or set its status to "void".

The above steps describe the "normal" process of the risk assessment solution. Let's try these first (and ignore that there is an error), to get rid of the error in the first place. If the above steps either cause problems or don't solve the issue, I'd suggest to delete the identified risk (technically). To do so, please perform the following steps:

  1. In the backend, have a look at the table EHHSSD_RSK_ROOT. You can search by Location (technical name: LOC_ROOT_KEY_REF), Hazard (technical name: HAZARD), Job (technical name: JOB_KEY_REF), and Operational Status (technical name: OPERATION_STATUS) to find the duplicate risk.
  2. Once you found the duplicate risk, copy the content of the column NodeID (technical name: DB_KEY, the first column in the result list). It's a purely technical identifier, a so-called UUID and should look anything like this: 670D114......32FBE7.
  3. I'm using the following example: (it's not a job-based risk, but it does not matter for the following steps):
  4. Go to the transaction BOBT. Now, it will get a bit technical - but you can do it 😉,
  5. In the upper-left corner, there is one input field, enter EHHSS_RISK and hit enter,
  6. Below the input field, there is a little button with a folder icon, click on it, and pick By Key -> ROOT,
  7. In the pop-up box, enter the key you copied in step 2, click on ok
  8. On the right-hand side of the screen, the risk is loaded. Select it from the list (a), click on the little edit button (b), click on the delete button (c), and finally, save (d).
  9. Check the app "Identify Risks", the risk(s) should no longer be visible:

     

Please let me know if the above suggestions/steps solved your issue.

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: In SAP Business One, what is the purpose of the Order Number field in the Accounting tab of purc
entry.content: Hi there! In SAP Business One, the Order Number field in the Accounting tab of Purchase Quotation and Purchase Order forms is typically populated manually by the user. This field is used to reference external order numbers provided by customers or vendors. Yes, this field can impact other areas of the system, such as linking purchase orders to sales orders or tracking specific transactions. And yes, you can generate reports using this field. You can use SAP Business One's Query Wizard or Query Generator tools to create custom reports that include the Order Number field.

entry.title: CDS Views 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content:
Introduction

This Quick Reference is a collection of new CDS functionality made available in ABAP 7.5x releases. This functionality is summarised in a way that will make sense to those familiar with SAP CDS. Links to the SAP Help  are also provided throughout to allow the reader to dive in deeper if desired. The version the functionality was released is also given to aid in determining if it is available in your system.

Contents

  1. CDS View Entity

  2. CDS Abstract Entity
  3. CDS Custom Entity
  4. CDS Projection views

 

1. CDS View Entity (7.50)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@view_entity_annot1]
[@view_entity_annot2]
...
[DEFINE] [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY view_entity
        [parameter_list]
         AS select_statement [;]...

Description

CDS view entities are non DDIC-based views. While they were introduced in 7.50 the old DDIC-based views (DEFINE VIEW ddic_based_view) only became obsolete in 7.56.

 

Extensions

EXTEND VIEW ENTITY (7.55)

Makes it possible to add new view fields to existing CDS view entities and CDS projection views by using CDS view entity extensions.

EXTEND VIEW ENTITY cds_view_entity | projection_view
       WITH
            [association1
             association2 ...]
            { select_list_extension }
            [GROUP BY field1, field2, ...  ]
            [EXCEPT | INTERSECT | UNION [ALL]  { ... }] [;]

 

ANNOTATE ENTITY (7.53)

Define CDS metadata extensions(type:DDLX) for any CDS entities with the exception of CDS table functions.

@Metadata.layer: #CORE|#LOCALIZATION|#INDUSTRY|#PARTNER|#CUSTOMER
   [@entity_annot1]
   [@entity_annot2]
   ...
   ANNOTATE ENTITY cds_entity WITH
     [ parameter_list]
     { [element_list] }[;]

 

Set Operators

UNION (7.56)       

Now supported in CDS view entities.

EXCEPT (7.56) 

Returns all rows of a SELECT statement that are not part of the result sets of the following SELECT statements.

INTERSECT (7.56)

Returns all distinct rows that are included in all result sets of multiple SELECT statements.

 

Functions

GET_NUMERIC_VALUE (7.56)

Conversion Function. Returns the numeric value of a currency or quantity without its currency or unit key.

 

  key carrid,
  key connid,
  key fldate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      price,
      currency,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      paymentsum,
      GET_NUMERIC_VALUE( paymentsum )  / GET_NUMERIC_VALUE( price )
                as number_of_bookings

 

CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT (7.56)

 Conversion Function. Converts the function argument(must have data type CURR and a currency key ) into a type DECFLOAT34.

 

  key id,
      cuky                              as currency,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      curr10_4                          as amount,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT(curr10_4) as curr_conv

 

SUBSTRING (7.57)

Function enhanced to accept literals, fields, parameters, expressions, and built-in functions as arguments pos and len.

SUBSTRING(arg, pos, len)     Substring arg from the position pos with length len. 

 

LEFT and RIGHT (7.57)

Functions enhanced to accept  literals, fields, parameters, expressions, and built-in functions as argument len.

LEFT(arg, len)                String of the length len with the len left characters of arg (ignoring the trailing blanks).

RIGHT(arg,len)              String of the length len with the len right characters of arg (ignoring the trailing blanks).

 

Types

Typed Literals (7.56) 

Now supported in CDS view entities. Not supported in the ON condition of a CDS association.

 

{
  key id,
      abap.char'aaa   '       as char_trailing_blanks,
      abap.sstring'aaa   '  as sstring_trailing_blanks,

      abap.string'aaa   '    as string_trailing_blanks,
      'aaa'                               as untyped_literal   }
where
  numc1 = abap.numc'1' and
   datn < abap.datn'20200101'

 

CDS Calculated Quantity (7.56)

A new type that is the result of a calculation using amount and/or quantity fields.

 

Application Session Variables (7.57)

In form $session.vname

vname

Value when accessed

bs_instance_id

Instance ID of the current business service instance. The returned value has the data type SSTRING.

bs_zone_id

Zone ID of the current business service instance. The returned value has the data type SSTRING.

 

Input Parameters (7.57)

abap.string now supported.

 

ABAP Reports

RUTDDLS_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES (7.56) 

Evaluates whether a migration from a CDS DDIC-based view to a CDS view entity is possible.

 RUTDDLSV2MIGRATION (7.56) 

Migrates CDS DDIC-based views to CDS view entities.

RUT_WHERE_USE_SQLVIEW (7.57)

Lists all repository objects that use CDS-managed DDIC views.

 

Annotations

Table Buffering Annotation (7.57).

Defines whether table buffering is allowed for a CDS view entity or not.

@AbapCatalog.entityBuffer.definitionAllowed: true|false

 

CDS entity buffer (7.57)

The actual buffering type can be defined by a separate ABAP tuning object called a CDS entity buffer with the DDL statement:

DEFINE VIEW ENTITY BUFFER ON cds_view_entity
                   LAYER CORE
                       | LOCALIZATION
                       | INDUSTRY
                       | PARTNER
                       | CUSTOMER
                   TYPE SINGLE
                      | {GENERIC NUMBER OF KEY ELEMENTS number}
                      | FULL
                      | NONE

Table buffering can be defined differently for the layers core, localization, industry, partner and customer.

 

Other

DISTINCT (7.56)

Available for SELECT statements in CDS view entities. Works like ABAP SELECT.

 

Reusing Expressions (7.56)

Reuse expressions defined in the SELECT list in other operand positions of the same CDS view entity.

 

key id                                 as field1,
    concat($projection.field1, 'x')    as field_reuse,

 

WHERE clause (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity DEMO_SALES_ORDER_WHERE
  as select from
    demo_sales_order
    association to demo_sales_bupa as _partner
      on $projection.id = _partner.id
    {
      key so_key,
          id,
          company_code,
          _partner.family_name
    }
    where
          _partner.family_name like    'S%'
      and length( _partner.family_name ) = abap.int1'4'
      and created_on between abap.dats'20200101' and abap.dats'20200401'
      and cast( _partner.birth_name as DEMO_BT_BIRTH_NAME
                preserving type )  =       abap.char'Meier'

 

 HAVING clause  (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity DEMO_CDS_VIEW_ENTITY_HAVING
  as select from sflight
  {
    concat_with_space(carrid, connid, 1) as ID,
    count(*)                             as col_count,
    seatsmax
  }
  group by
    carrid,
    connid,
    seatsmax
  having
    seatsmax > abap.int2'500'

 

Searched Case Expression (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity demo_cds_searched_case_ve
  as select from
    spfli
    {
      key carrid,
      key connid,
          distance,
          distid,
          case
            when distance >= 2000 then 'long-haul flight'
            when distance >= 1000 and
                 distance <  2000 then 'medium-haul flight'
            when distance <  1000 then 'short-haul flight'
                                  else 'error'
          end as flight_type
    }
    where
      distid = 'MI'

 

2. CDS Abstract Entity (7.53)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[DEFINE] [ROOT] ABSTRACT ENTITY abstract_entity
         [parameter_list]
          element_list[;]

Description

Works like a structure definition but nothing is created in DDIC and has annotations that can be evaluated with class CL_DD_DDL_ANNOTATION_SERVICE.

 

EXTEND ABSTRACT ENTITY (7.56)

Allows extension of existing abstract entity

EXTEND ABSTRACT ENTITY cds_abstract_entity WITH  { element_list_extension }

 

To-Parent Association Without ON (7.56)

The ON condition can be left out of a “to-parent” if the association target is also a CDS abstract entity.

 

define abstract entity DEMO_CDS_ABSTRACT_CHILD
{
  key key_field  : abap.char(1);
      data_field    : abap.int4;
      _to_parent  : association to parent DEMO_CDS_ABSTRACT_COMPOSITION  
                     on _to_parent.key_field = $projection.key_field;
           <- Last line can be omitted
}

 

 3. CDS Custom Entity (7.54)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy : 'class_name']
[DEFINE] [ROOT] CUSTOM ENTITY custom_entity
         [parameter_list]
          element_list[;]

Description

No DDIC entries created and has annotations that can be evaluated with class CL_DD_DDL_ANNOTATION_SERVICE.

Used in RAP as basis of unmanaged queries.

 

 EXTEND CUSTOM ENTITY (7.57)

Makes it possible to add new elements to existing CDS custom entities by using CDS custom entity extensions.

EXTEND CUSTOM ENTITY CustomEntity WITH { ElementListExtension }

 

4. CDS Projection Views

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@proj_view_annot1]
[@proj_view_annot2]
...
DEFINE [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY projection_view
   [ PROVIDER CONTRACT provider_contract]               [From 7.56]
    AS PROJECTION ON cds_entity [AS alias_name]
   [association1 association2 ...]                                 [From 7.55]
   [redefined_assoc1 redefined_assoc2 ...]                    [From 7.56]
      { projection_list }
   [WHERE cds_cond]                                              [From 7.58 for Transactional Interfaces ]

Description

A special CDS view entity that is based on another CDS view entity (called projected entity). CDS projection views adapt a CDS data model for service-specific use cases.

 

Associations (7.55)

It is possible to add new, local, read-only associations association1, association2 to a projection view. Read-only associations in projection views can only be exposed but they cannot be used in path expressions to expose elements from new associations.

 

REDEFINE ASSOCIATION (7.56) 

For Projection views it is now possible to redefine an association. In this way, it is possible to modify an association from the underlying view. 

... REDEFINE ASSOCIATION [source.]_ProjAssoc [ filter] [AS _RedefinedName] redirection  ...

 

PROVIDER CONTRACT (7.56) 

For Projection views it is now possible to specify a provider contract specifies in which scenario a CDS projection view is used.  Provider contract options available:

  • TRANSACTIONAL_QUERY (7.56)             [Default if not provided]
  • TRANSACTIONAL_INTERFACE (7.57)    [Use for Transactional Interfaces]
  • ANALYTICAL_QUERY (7.57)                       [Use for Transient View Entities]

 

CDS Transactional Query (7.56)

Uses DEFINE [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with  provider contract “TRANSACTIONAL_QUERY”.

Transactional queries are intended for modelling the projection layer of a RAP business object. A transactional projection view must always be part of a business object. That means it must either be a root entity itself, or it must be part of a composition tree that contains a root entity

 

CDS Transactional Interface (7.57)

Uses DEFINE VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with  provider contract “TRANSACTIONAL_INTERFACE”.

CDS transactional interfaces serve as stable public interface layer in a CDS data model. They are typically used in the context of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model to provide the basis for a RAP BO interface. 

WHERE  (7.58) condition allowed for Transactional Interfaces.

 

CDS Analytical Projection Views (7.57)

Uses DEFINE TRANSIENT VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with provider contract “ANALYTICAL_QUERY”.

A CDS DDL that is intended for modeling analytical queries within a CDS data model. A CDS analytical projection view defines an analytical query in its element_list.

The projected entity cds_entity can be an analytical cube view(7.57) or an analytical dimension view(7.58). One base view can serve as a basis for multiple analytical projection views.

entry.title: Re: Is BusinessObjects Still Relevant in 2025?
entry.content:
Dear Dallas,

I can totally support your view on this matter, when I see my Bobj customers to "copy" webi reports into the now more fancy MS Power Bi - I could roll my eyes... litterally 😉  - Well there are still enough Webi reports in our Repository - so one less - recreated painfully in PBI  - without standards -  like set in the BOBJ Universes - let the people play if they want to... MS Marketing is doing it's best. But  FUD is  a topic that counts - lately I met an ex colleague - he knew I am still working with BOBJ - "But isn't it "dead" already?" he asked me! ...  Well that hurts! I confess! 

Well for the next BOBJ Versions we have clear visions! Looking forward to BOBJ Future!

 

yours

 

WoBi
